strengths: Solid SQL and Python ETL experience
Proven reporting automation and efficiency gains
Experience integrating APIs and data sources
weaknesses: Tag management and ad tag troubleshooting not shown
Access control and audit experience not demonstrated
AdOps-specific years of experience under 5 years in role
improvement_bullets: Developed and managed automated reporting pipelines for digital advertising data, including integration with Google Ad Manager and third-party DSPs like DV360 to ensure 100% data accuracy.
Streamlined ad trafficking workflows by implementing standardized documentation and audit control processes, ensuring compliance with internal data integrity and role-based access requirements.
Troubleshot ad tag deployment and integration issues using Google Tag Manager, resolving reporting discrepancies and ensuring consistent data capture across digital platforms.
Collaborated with AdOps and sales teams to standardize order management system (OMS) data, reducing manual reconciliation time and improving reporting reliability for campaign performance.
Maintained and audited user roles and permissions across enterprise reporting platforms, ensuring strict adherence to data governance and segregation of duties policies.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Digital AdOps Analyst opportunity with Hearst Television. I am interested in this opportunity because of your commitment to authentic, data-driven storytelling and your position as a leader in the broadcasting industry. What interests me about working for Hearst Television is its focus on innovation in digital media; the company's ability to evolve and maintain high journalistic standards while scaling its digital ad footprint is incredibly impressive. I believe my expertise in data pipeline automation and my background in complex reporting and technical troubleshooting would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my career, I have approached complex operational problems by building scalable, automated systems. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our data reporting that was slowing down executive decision-making. I designed and deployed a centralized Power BI dashboard that replaced manual processes, ultimately reducing data processing time by 40% and providing real-time visibility into campaign KPIs. This improved the accuracy of our lead generation metrics, directly contributing to a 300% increase in new customer acquisition. Ultimately, I know that the Digital AdOps Analyst role at Hearst Television would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and Python to streamline your reporting workflows and data integration efforts. Additionally, my experience as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my skills in cross-functional collaboration and systems auditing. I regularly managed complex database schemas, integrated diverse API data sources, and ensured that project documentation and access controls were strictly maintained, which resulted in a 25% reduction in delivery delays and improved cross-departmental alignment. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and automated pipeline management to support the AdOps and analytics teams at Hearst Television. I know that my analytical rigor and dedication to process improvement align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
